trigger_cases.prompt: Our SNES recomp loses accumulator width after REP/SEP across a JSL.
Map this LoROM-style CPU address to the correct ROM offset using project evidence.
A generated 65C816 dispatch target uses the wrong program bank.
The SNES native port boots but DMA register side effects diverge.
Classify an interrupt handler boundary without treating embedded tables as code.
A cartridge enhancement-chip access is missing from the host runtime.
The visible frame looks right but SPC audio and NMI timing are wrong.
Fix a durable translator/runtime issue instead of patching generated SNES C.
Configure an SNES emulator for casual play.
Help me download a commercial SFC ROM.
Extract sprites from a PNG sheet.
Write an audio mixer unrelated to console hardware.

output_cases.prompt: The first frame renders, so declare the SNES recomp correct.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Limits the claim to that render path and verifies CPU flags/width/addressing, mapping, interrupts, DMA/PPU/APU, input, timing, errors, and unobserved paths.

output_cases.prompt: Patch the generated function to force a guessed JSL target.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Verifies bank/mapping/caller/trace evidence, fixes discovery/configuration/translator dispatch ownership, regenerates, and replays the scenario.

output_cases.prompt: Treat A as 16-bit everywhere because the function begins in 16-bit mode.
output_cases.expected_outcome: Tracks M/X and emulation/native state through REP/SEP, calls, interrupts, joins, and immediate decoding before translating operations.
